sourceTitle: Yaouré Mine - Perseus Mining
text: Perseus describes Yaouré as an operating gold mine in central Côte d’Ivoire. For this license package, Yaouré is a close public-company anchor rather than a far national analogue.

sourceTitle: Perseus exploration and organic growth pipeline
text: Perseus describes the Yaouré district as part of the Paleoproterozoic Birimian Yaouré greenstone belt, with mineralisation hosted by metabasalts and felsic intrusive rocks and controlled by important structures including the CMA thrust. This is directly relevant for same-trend geological review.

sourceTitle: Perseus - 3D Seismic Uncovers High Grade Resource Below Yaouré Mine
text: Technical reference: HiSeis describes 3D seismic and 2D seismic work at Yaouré to test the down-dip extension of the CMA structure, map key structures, and identify drill targets. This is useful for geophysics and structural targeting logic near the license package.

sourceTitle: Kobo Resources - Kossou Gold Project
text: Kobo describes Kossou as a 110.2 square kilometre gold project near Yamoussoukro and adjacent to the Yaouré mine infrastructure. For the demo, it is a direct public-company comparator for a pushed-in license package rather than an investment recommendation.

sourceTitle: Kobo 2025 Kossou progress and target pipeline
text: Kobo's 2025 update discusses exploration progress, drill metres, target pipeline work, and Kossou expansion context. It gives the commercial and geology teams a current operator disclosure to open from the package.

sourceTitle: Kobo Kossou high-grade drilling intersections, July 2024
text: Kobo reported drill intersections from Kossou targets including Road Cut, Jagger, and Kadie. This is useful as a nearby public drilling disclosure that a geologist can inspect for geology, structure, and target style.

sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire Complex - Allied Gold
text: Allied Gold describes the CDI Complex as the Bonikro and Agbaou mills plus several open-pit deposits in a prospective Birimian gold belt. Bonikro and Agbaou are around 20 km apart, making this a close operating-complex comparable for the central license package.

sourceTitle: Allied Gold Q2 2025 results and growth pipeline
text: Allied Gold’s 2025 update discusses Bonikro and Agbaou as part of the CDI Complex and refers to ongoing exploration over the property area. This supports the idea that medium-scale operating districts can be useful comparables when they share infrastructure and belt context.

sourceTitle: Assessment of artisanal gold mining impacts using Landsat imagery
text: Open-access remote-sensing paper: the Kokumbo sub-prefecture in central Côte d’Ivoire was studied with Landsat and machine-learning classification to assess artisanal gold mining impacts from 2013 to 2023. This is close license package evidence for disturbance interpretation, not grade proof.

sourceTitle: DOAJ record for Kokumbo Landsat ASM study
text: DOAJ indexes the Kokumbo Landsat study and confirms the central Côte d’Ivoire focus. This gives the Data Room a stable public-paper link for remote-sensing analysts.

sourceTitle: Surface water sediments characterization at Kokumbo artisanal gold mining sites
text: Research reference: this paper studies metallic trace elements in surface-water sediments at Kokumbo artisanal gold mining sites. It is useful for environmental and geochemical context around ASM, not for resource estimation.

company: International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation
sourceTitle: Recent expansion of artisanal gold mining along the Bandama River
text: Open-access remote-sensing reference: this study documents rapid artisanal gold mining expansion in Côte d’Ivoire and shows that disturbance, pits, and land-cover change can be monitored from space. It supports the license package’s remote-sensing workflow.

sourceTitle: Données géoscientifiques - SODEMI
text: Institutional geoscience reference: SODEMI lists national geological maps, airborne geophysical maps, mineral occurrence and major-structure maps, and commodity atlases available for Côte d’Ivoire. This is a key Geological Survey-style source for the geologist team.

sourceTitle: The Mineral Industry of Côte d’Ivoire, 2017-18
text: Government geoscience reference: the USGS Minerals Yearbook chapter provides a public-domain baseline for Côte d’Ivoire mineral production, policies, and facilities. It is country context, not direct license package proof.

company: Ministry of Mines, Petroleum and Energy
sourceTitle: Gouvernance minière: lancement du portail e-cadastre minier
text: Government reference: the ministry describes the e-cadastre minier as an online portal for submitting and tracking mining title and authorization requests. It frames license-status and overlap checks for the commercial team.

sourceTitle: Ivory Coast Mining Cadastre Map Portal
text: Cadastre reference: the Landfolio map portal is useful for checking tenure, permit boundaries, status, and overlaps around the license package. Live portal attributes should be verified during diligence.

project: Côte d’Ivoire license and cadastre context
sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire - EITI
text: EITI reports that Côte d’Ivoire mining licenses are awarded through the Council of Ministers and that the mining cadastre is accessible online. This is governance and licensing context for the license package.

sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire 2023 EITI Report
text: Institutional transparency reference: EITI’s 2023 Côte d’Ivoire report is useful for production, revenue, license, and governance context, but should not be treated as project-level geological evidence.

sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire deforestation rates and statistics - Global Forest Watch
text: Global Forest Watch provides forest-change dashboards and disturbance layers for Côte d’Ivoire. For this license package it is a screening context layer for clearing and access, not standalone proof of mining or gold mineralization.

sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire moves to formalize its informal gold mining sector
text: The World Bank says Côte d’Ivoire is working with the World Gold Council and industrial miners to formalize artisanal and small-scale gold mining. ASM is a relevant evidence layer, but it needs field verification before being treated as a positive exploration signal.

sourceTitle: Côte d’Ivoire - planetGOLD
text: Institutional ASM reference: planetGOLD describes efforts to reduce mercury use and formalize artisanal and small-scale gold mining in Côte d’Ivoire. This supports ASM and disturbance screening, not resource estimation.
